subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Logical chain of introduction â methods â conclusion ensures coherence
Clear organization via headings, sub-headings, numbered figures and tables
Mostly relevant content with concise explanations supporting the aim
weaknesses: Occasional digressions and dense blocks of equations interrupt flow
Formatting glitches (misaligned tables, page breaks) detract from seamless presentation
Conclusion is abrupt and citation style is inconsistent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the work firmly in band 3: coherent logical development with clear structure, but minor lapses prevent a band 4 award. The organization is strong yet marred by formatting issues, and the content is relevant and mostly concise though occasional digressions and an incomplete conclusion limit further credit.

strengths: Consistent use of coordinate notation, bisector gradients and algorithmic tables
Effective integration of multiple representations (pitch diagrams, Voronoi overlay, network graphs)
Generally clear explanations, defined symbols, and consistent rounding practices
weaknesses: Typographical sign error in the distance formula and some inconsistent subscripts
Overflowing tables and duplicated figure numbers harm clarity
Unconventional notation in listing edge pairs can confuse readers
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The communication is mostly clear, with appropriate notation throughout and effective use of representations, placing it in band 3. Minor errors in notation and layout inconsistencies prevent the consistency and sophistication required for band 4.

strengths: Strong personal rationale rooted in lifelong fandom and playing experience
Independent thinking evident in choice and justification of mathematical approaches
Creative initiative shown by proposing thoughtful extensions and model adaptations
weaknesses: Once chosen, standard mathematical techniques limit the degree of originality
Modelling remains relatively straightforward and does not reach exceptional innovation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation shows significant personal engagementâclear personal approach, independent thinking, and creativityâmeriting band 2. However, techniques are standard once selected, so it stops short of the outstanding engagement required for band 3.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on data limitations, tactical assumptions and algorithmic choices
Basic critical analysis in rejecting the Travelling Salesman approach and discussing ecological validity
Consistent links between mathematical findings and football understanding
weaknesses: Reflection lacks quantification of uncertainty or deeper critical rigour
Opportunities to strengthen analysis (error bounds, windspeed conversion) are not exploited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reflection is meaningful and consistently linked to understanding, fulfilling band 2. It demonstrates genuine critique but lacks the depth, sustained critical analysis and quantification needed for band 3.

strengths: Relevant use of Voronoi geometry and Dijkstraâs algorithm at course level
Calculations are mostly correct with only minor errors, demonstrating good accuracy
Clear conceptual understanding of methods and purpose of mathematical tools
weaknesses: Sign error in the distance formula and omitted justification for gradient derivation
Lacks mathematical justification for graph connectivity based on formation extrapolation
Quantitative trade-off analysis (sensitivity or error discussion) is missing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is used effectively at the course level, with good accuracy and understanding throughout, placing the work in band 4. Minor errors and omissions in rigorous justification and quantitative discussion prevent a higher award.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing a personal football moment, extracting data from video, and deciding to mesh Voronoi geometry with Dijkstraâs algorithm demonstrates clear independent thought beyond a textbook approach; however, the techniques themselves are standard once chosen, so evidence is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

Explanations of algorithm steps, justification for excluding edges, and discussion of formation assumptions display good conceptual grasp across the exploration.  Some deeper theoretical justification of Voronoi properties is absent, so the strand remains at âgood understanding throughoutâ rather than âthorough with rigourâ.

Numerical values are mostly sensible and consistent with diagrams; calculations for bisectors and distances are reproduced correctly apart from a sign typo in the distance formula (minus instead of plus).  Such slips are minor, so the work fits âcorrect with minor errorsâ, band 4.

Most material directly supports the aims: Voronoi construction, distance calculations, Dijkstraâs algorithm and reflections on realistic passing routes.  Explanatory football anecdotes and repeated algorithm screenshots add some bloat, but overall the text is relevant and not excessively long for a 16-page IA, satisfying the ârelevant and mostly conciseâ descriptor.

The student routinely uses coordinate notation, perpendicular-bisector gradients, distance formulae, graph-theory symbols and algorithmic tables.  Apart from a typographical sign error in the distance formula and a few inconsistent subscripts, notation is appropriate and sustained, matching the âmostly appropriateâ descriptor.

Explanations, diagrams and tables are generally clear; symbol meanings are introduced and units are shown.  Minor inconsistencies (some tables overflow, two figure numbers duplicated) keep it from full consistency but the work still meets the âclear and mostly consistentâ standard.

Voronoi diagrams, perpendicular-bisector algebra and Dijkstraâs algorithm all lie within or slightly beyond HL level and are relevant throughout.  The mathematics is deployed purposefully rather than ornamentally, so ârelevant mathematics at course level used effectivelyâ is justified.

Diagrams of the pitch with points, a Voronoi overlay, network graphs with edge weights, and summary tables give several complementary representations of the mathematics.  They are clearly integrated into the argument, though not pushed to a highly sophisticated level (e.g., no dynamic visualisations), so an âeffective useâ judgement is appropriate.

The student comments on limitations of snapshot data, windspeed assumptions, and tactical realism, evidencing reflection that is âmeaningfulâ across the work.  The discussion is insightful but not deeply critical or sustained enough for the top band.

Applying two distinct mathematical models to a real match situation, deciding to remove central-pitch edges for âecological validityâ, and proposing extensions reveal creativity and initiative, yet the modelling remains relatively straightforward and does not reach the âoutstandingâ threshold.

The exploration follows a recognisable chain: introduction â data collection â construction of Voronoi diagram â graph-theory model â algorithms â conclusion.  Each section builds naturally on the previous one, so the reader seldom feels lost.  Minor lapses (e.g., occasional digressions when explaining football context) interrupt the flow, preventing an âexcellentâ judgement but overall coherence is solid and clearly above âsomeâ development.

There is basic but genuine critique: rejection of the travelling-salesman idea, acknowledgement of algorithmic shortcomings, and analysis of why central-pitch passes are unrealistic.  The critique goes beyond description but lacks the depth and rigour of band 3.

Reflections consistently link findings back to football understandingâhow City could defend and counter, why wide play is preferable, and how formations influence routesâshowing a clear connection to understanding, but they stop short of demonstrating âenhancedâ or transformative insight.

Headings, sub-headings, numbered figures and tables give the work a clear introductionâbodyâconclusion structure.  Pages are in logical order and the reader can locate definitions, data, working and results quickly.  A few formatting glitches (mis-aligned giant tables, page breaks) stop it from reaching the seamless level expected in band 4.

The rationale is rooted in the studentâs lifelong support of Manchester United and personal playing experience, and the investigation is steered by those interests throughout.  This personal drive is evident and shapes the exploration, fulfilling the band-2 descriptor.

annotations.comment: The discussion of estimation error in plotting positions is good, but the potential margin of error is not quantified. Consider estimating uncertainty bounds to strengthen your analysis.
The integration of GeoGebra to scale and generate the Voronoi diagram is effective and demonstrates an appropriate use of multiple mathematical representations.
The notation \(\overline{(AB, AC, BC,\dots)}\) is unconventional and ambiguous. Explicitly list each pair or use set notation \(\{AB,AC,BC,\ldots\}\) for clarity.
The derivation of the perpendicular gradient formula lacks justification. Include a brief explanation of why \(m_{\perp}= -\frac{x_1-x_2}{y_1-y_2}\) follows from the product of slopes being \(-1\).
In the worked example, readability suffers from dense block equations. Consider spacing out each step and clearly labelling them to improve clarity.
Coordinates in Fig. 5 are consistently rounded to two decimal places, which is good practice. Remember to specify the rounding rule and include units (metres) for full precision.
The rationale for adding extrapolated vertices based on a 4-2-1-3 formation is stated but not justified mathematically. Explain how formation positions influence graph connectivity.
The distance formula is written as \(\sqrt{(x_1-x_2)^2 - (y_1-y_2)^2}\), but it should be \(\sqrt{(x_1-x_2)^2 + (y_1-y_2)^2}\). Correct the sign error.
The aim statement is very long and combines multiple objectives. Consider breaking it into concise, separate aims each focusing on specific variables and desired outcomes to enhance clarity.
The studentâs personal rationale rooted in lifelong fandom and playing experience provides outstanding engagement with the topic and drives the exploration authentically.
The plan description omits clarity on how the data extrapolation to the whole pitch will account for dynamic formations. State explicit assumptions or include sensitivity analysis.
Several in-text citations (e.g., âTotal Football, 2018â) lack corresponding entries in the Works Cited. Ensure every citation has a full reference.
The discussion of windspeedâs inverse effect on pass distance is insightful. To deepen reflection, convert mph to m/s and quantify how much maximum pass length might change.
The decision to abandon the Travelling Salesman approach in favor of Dijkstraâs demonstrates significant independent thinking and justification beyond standard coursework.
In the algorithm description, specify whether the graph is directed or undirected and justify that choice in the football-passing context.
The Dijkstra tables on page 10 are too wide and overflow the margin, which detracts from readability. Reformat tables or split them to improve presentation.
Table formatting in Fig. 14 shows misaligned columns and inconsistent colspan usage. Ensure each cell is clearly aligned with appropriate headers.
The model adaptation by removing central passes for ecological validity shows deep critical reflection and aligns the mathematics with real-world tactics.
The trade-off between increased route length and tactical realism when switching to E-B-G-I-K is noted, but a quantitative discussion of this compromise would enhance the analysis.
The conclusion abruptly ends mid-sentence. Revise to include a complete summary of findings, limitations, and possible implications for coaching practice.
The extensions section outlines thoughtful future directions, showing initiative and ability to generalize the investigation beyond the specific match context.
Works Cited entries lack a consistent referencing style and often omit publication details. Adopt a standard format (e.g., APA or MLA) and apply it uniformly.
